message:
fix(tcp): wake surviving waiter on drop-time re-registration failure

When one half of a split TCP stream is dropped, clear_waiter_on_drop
re-arms the registration for the surviving half. If set_interest or
update_waker fails (e.g. one-shot backends that lost the slot), the
surviving waiter was left stranded with no registration and no wake
signal — it would block indefinitely.

Changes:
- Always call set_interest on drop (removed the interest-unchanged
  skip) because one-shot backends may need re-arming even if the
  interest bits don't change after removing the dropped half
- On set_interest or update_waker failure, wake remaining waiters
  so their poll paths can attempt fresh registration or surface
  terminal errors to callers
- When no registration exists but a waiter remains, wake it so it
  can discover the missing registration
- Added fail_modify_on_call() to test mock reactor for fault injection
- Added regression test:
  dropping_write_half_wakes_survivor_when_reregistration_fails —
  uses CountingWaker to verify the read half's waker is invoked
  when the drop-time modify call is injected to fail

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
